clauses.text: This Agreement is entered into between [CLIENT_NAME] ("Client") and [PROVIDER_NAME] ("Service Provider").
This Agreement governs the provision of [PROJECT_NAME] by the Service Provider to the Client.

The Service Provider shall provide technical consulting services described in [SERVICES_CONTRACTED], culminating in the report(s) described in the Deliverables Clause. The Service Provider's advice is based on the information provided by the Client; the Client remains solely responsible for decisions to implement, and the manner of implementing, any recommendation.
The Service Provider shall deliver the Deliverables to the Client on or before [DELIVERY_DATE].
The Services shall take place on [DELIVERY_DATE] at , subject to any change confirmed in writing by both Parties.
The Client shall pay the Service Provider [TOTAL_VALUE] for the Services, payable via [PAYMENT_METHOD] according to the schedule agreed between the Parties.
If the Client fails to pay any amount due by its due date, the Service Provider may charge interest on the overdue amount at [LATE_PAYMENT_RATE] per month.

The Service Provider shall provide up to [INCLUDED_FOLLOW_UP_MEETINGS] follow-up meetings to discuss the Deliverables at no additional charge. Additional meetings shall be charged at [ADDITIONAL_MEETING_RATE] per meeting.

Where the Client requests support implementing the Service Provider's recommendations, the Service Provider shall provide up to [IMPLEMENTATION_SUPPORT_HOURS] hours of assistance at [IMPLEMENTATION_SUPPORT_RATE] per hour beyond that allotment.
The Client shall reimburse the Service Provider's reasonable, pre-approved travel expenses in addition to [TOTAL_VALUE].
Where the Services include training, the Service Provider shall provide [TRAINING_HOURS] hours of training on the Deliverables' recommendations.
Reports requested beyond those described in the Deliverables Clause shall be charged at [ADDITIONAL_REPORT_FEE] per report.
The Parties shall meet according to [MEETING_SCHEDULE] to review progress on the Services.

Each Party shall keep confidential any non-public information disclosed by the other Party in connection with this Agreement and shall not disclose it without the disclosing Party's prior written consent.

Upon full payment of [TOTAL_VALUE], the Client receives ownership of the report(s) delivered under this Agreement. The Service Provider retains ownership of its pre-existing methodologies, frameworks, and general know-how used in preparing the report(s), and may reuse such methodologies, frameworks, and know-how in engagements with other clients.
Neither Party shall be liable to the other for any indirect, incidental, or consequential loss arising from this Agreement.
Each Party's total liability arising from this Agreement shall not exceed [TOTAL_VALUE], except for loss arising from gross negligence, willful misconduct, or fraud.

Neither Party shall be liable for a failure to perform its obligations to the extent that failure results from an event beyond that Party's reasonable control that could not have been avoided through reasonable diligence. The affected Party shall notify the other Party without undue delay.
This Agreement shall be governed by [APPLICABLE_LAW]. Any dispute shall be submitted to [DISPUTE_RESOLUTION_MECHANISM] before litigation, except where interim injunctive relief is required.

The Parties have executed this Agreement as of [SIGNATURE_DATE]. Ambiguity: none — the advisory disclaimer explicitly locates implementation responsibility with the Client, directly addressing Step 2 risk #1 within the primary Consulting Scope Clause rather than burying it elsewhere. Duplication: Follow-up Meetings vs. Implementation Support kept distinct — the former is discussion of the report, the latter is hands-on assistance executing its recommendations, genuinely separate services. Loophole closed: Intellectual Property Clause explicitly separates report ownership (transferred to Client) from underlying methodology/know-how (retained by consultant), closing a gap that could otherwise force the consultant to abandon reusable expertise for every client engagement. Contradiction: none. Missing protection: Additional Reports Clause added given this Contract Type's report-based deliverable structure, distinct from quantity-based fields used in build-oriented Contract Types (e.g., screens, pages). Complexity: minimal, correctly reflecting that this Contract Type is advisory rather than deliverable-build-based, hence no Acceptance Testing or Source Code Ownership Blocks are needed. Enforceability: follow-up meeting and implementation support hours both explicitly capped with defined overage rates. Attack: Client implements the consultant's advice poorly and sues the consultant for the resulting business failure — mitigated by the explicit disclaimer in the Consulting Scope Clause placing implementation responsibility with the Client. Attack: Consultant refuses to ever reuse a general framework developed during the engagement, or conversely, Client claims exclusive rights to the consultant's general methodology — mitigated by Intellectual Property Clause's explicit split between report ownership and retained methodology. Attack: Client expects unlimited follow-up calls indefinitely after the engagement ends — mitigated by Follow-up Meetings' explicit cap and per-meeting overage rate. No further gaps found. Version 1.0.0 — Self-Review and Red Team passed — Pending Human Legal Review.
